name: Acarbose
summary: Acarbose is tracked for Inhibits intestinal alpha-glucosidases, delaying carbohydrate digestion and postprandial glucose absorption with conservative evidence framing. Treat dosing and outcomes as review-gated unless PMID-backed human evidence is present.
description: Acarbose is tracked for Inhibits intestinal alpha-glucosidases, delaying carbohydrate digestion and postprandial glucose absorption with conservative evidence framing. Treat dosing and outcomes as review-gated unless PMID-backed human evidence is present.
contraindications: hypoglycemia risk when combined with sulfonylureas/insulin.
dosage: up to 100 mg three times daily in UKPDS 44; prescription-only context
typical_dosage: up to 100 mg three times daily in UKPDS 44; prescription-only context
buying_criteria: avoid proprietary blends and unsupported disease claims
